arts.ttl: Taty Castellanos Enchants as Sensational Lazio Smash Five Past Hopeless Monza
arts.sum: After a run of four winless matches at home, Lazio roared back by demolishing Monza by five goals to one at the Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Lazio Starting Lineup Marco Baroni confirmed his attacking choices as Taty Castellanos spearheaded the 4-2-3-1 formation, supported by Gustav Isasksen, Boulaye Dia and Mattia Zaccagni. arts.ttl: Serie A | Lazio 5-1 Monza: Match Report
arts.sum: Lazio delivered a resounding performance at the Stadio Olimpico, dismantling a struggling Monza side 5-1 in a match that saw Pedro bag a brace, alongside goals from Adam Marusic, Fisayo Dele-Bashiru, and Taty Castellanos, who celebrated his first Serie A home goal since September. The post Serie A | Lazio 5-1 Monza: Match Report appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.sum: Fenerbahce are reportedly interested in Lazio midfielder Matias Vecino as they look to bolster Jose Mourinho’s squad. The Background The 33-year-old has been plying his trade in Italian football since joining Fiorentina in 2013. After representing Cagliari, Empoli and Inter Milan, the midfielder signed for Lazio on a free transfer in the summer of 2022. arts.ttl: Lazio’s Gila Postpones Contract Renewal: Reasons and Scenarios
arts.sum: Mario Gila has become a Galactico at Lazio, finding his Real Madrid-level dimension in Rome. The explosion of this bright star has been nothing short of remarkable. Corriere dello Sport detail how Gila is one of the few defenders capable of shifting the axis of matches, as demonstrated recently in Cagliari. arts.sum: Today we remember wins against Milan and Inter, a controversial draw with Juventus, ridiculous refereeing in a match against Bari, and the tragic death of Fantoni II Matches of the Week Date: Sunday, February 3, 1957 Venue: Stadio Comunale, Turin Fixture: Juventus Lazio 3-3 Lazio denied win in Turin by dubious penalty and a controversially […] The post This Week In Lazio History: February 3-9 appeared first on The Laziali.

arts.ttl: Lazio vs Monza, in a bid to stay in fourth place
arts.sum: Lazio haven’t won any of their last four home league games (D2 L2), after five home wins in a row beforehand; the Biancocelesti last had a longer run back in 2016 (five). Lazio are unbeaten in their five Serie A matches against Monza (W3 D2); only against five teams, none of which are currently in Serie A, have the Biancocelesti played more games in the competition without losing (Frosinone, Mantova, Reggiana, Sampierdarenese and Spezia).
